BoundsBody.description: Bounds payload for offline bundle requests and responses.

CreateWalkSessionRequestBody.description: Request payload for creating a walk session.

CreateWalkSessionResponseBody.description: Response payload for walk session creation.

DeleteOfflineBundleResponseBody.description: Response payload for deleting an offline bundle.

DescriptorsResponse.description: Response payload for the descriptor registries snapshot.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the snapshot was assembled.

EnrichmentProvenanceRecordBody.description: JSON record payload for admin enrichment provenance reporting.

ExploreCatalogueResponse.description: Response payload for the explore catalogue snapshot.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the snapshot was assembled.

ListEnrichmentProvenanceQuery.description: Query parameters for enrichment provenance reporting.

before.description:
Optional exclusive cursor.

Accepts either bare `RFC3339` or `RFC3339|UUID` for `(importedAt,id)`.
Bare timestamps are interpreted as `(timestamp, Uuid::max())`.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 50, maximum 200.

ListEnrichmentProvenanceResponseBody.description: Response payload for enrichment provenance reporting.

nextBefore.description:
Exclusive `RFC3339|UUID` cursor for the next page.

Clients should round-trip this value into the `before` query
parameter to continue pagination.

ListOfflineBundlesQuery.description: Query parameters for listing offline bundles.

ListOfflineBundlesResponseBody.description: Response payload for listing offline bundles.

LoginRequest.description:
Login request body for `POST /api/v1/login`.

Example JSON:
`{"username":"admin","password":"password"}`

NoteRequest.description: Request payload for creating or updating a note.

OfflineBundleResponse.description: Response payload for an offline bundle manifest.

PaginatedUsersResponse.description: OpenAPI schema for the paginated users response envelope.

limit.description: Effective page size for this response.

PaginationLinksSchema.description: OpenAPI schema for pagination links in `GET /api/v1/users` responses.

next.description: URL for the next page, when a following page exists.

prev.description: URL for the previous page, when an earlier page exists.

self.description: Canonical URL for the current page.

PreferencesRequest.description: Request payload for updating user preferences.

ProgressRequest.description: Request payload for updating route progress.

ProvenanceBoundsBody.description: Bounds payload for one provenance record.

RouteAnnotationsResponse.description: Response payload aggregating notes and progress for a route.

RouteNoteResponse.description: Response payload for a route note.

UpsertOfflineBundleRequestBody.description: Request payload for creating or updating an offline bundle manifest.

UpsertOfflineBundleResponseBody.description: Response payload for upserting an offline bundle.

WalkCompletionSummaryResponseBody.description: Completion summary payload returned when a walk is complete.

ZoomRangeBody.description: Zoom payload for offline bundle requests and responses.

crate.domain.Error.description:
OpenAPI schema for [`crate::domain::Error`].

API error response payload with machine-readable code and human-readable
message.

traceId.description: Correlation identifier for tracing this error across systems.

crate.domain.ErrorCode.description:
OpenAPI schema for [`crate::domain::ErrorCode`].

Stable machine-readable error codes returned in API error responses.

crate.domain.InterestThemeId.description:
OpenAPI schema for [`crate::domain::InterestThemeId`].

Interest theme identifiers are UUIDs serialized as strings.

crate.domain.User.description:
OpenAPI schema for [`crate::domain::User`].

Application user with stable identifier and display name.

createdAt.description: Creation timestamp used as the first users pagination key.

displayName.description:
Display name shown to other users.

Schema constraints: 3–32 characters, alphanumeric plus spaces and
underscores. The domain layer additionally validates that the value
is non-empty when trimmed.

id.description:
Stable user identifier.

Matches the domain `User` invariant of being a UUID.

crate.domain.UserInterests.description:
OpenAPI schema for [`crate::domain::UserInterests`].

User interest selections with theme identifiers.

revision.description: Shared aggregate revision after the interests update.

info.description: HTTP interface for session-authenticated access and health probes.
info.title: Wildside backend API

parameters.description: Number of records to return, default 50, max 200
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Exclusive cursor RFC3339 or RFC3339|UUID for importedAt/id ordering; bare timestamps map to RFC3339|ffffffff-ffff-ffff-ffff-ffffffffffff
parameters.name: before

get.summary: List persisted enrichment provenance records for admin reporting.

get.description: Return all descriptor registries for the Progressive Web App (PWA). Example request: GET /api/v1/catalogue/descriptors
Return the explore catalogue snapshot for the Progressive Web App (PWA) landing page. Example request: GET /api/v1/catalogue/explore

post.description:
Uses the centralized `Error` type so clients get a consistent
error schema across all endpoints.
post.summary: Authenticate user and establish a session.

get.summary: List offline bundle manifests for the authenticated user and device.

post.summary: Create or update an offline bundle manifest.

get.summary: Fetch notes and progress for a route.

post.summary: Create or update a note for the route.

put.summary: Update route progress for the authenticated user.

parameters.description: Number of users to return, default 20, max 100
parameters.name: limit

put.summary: Update the authenticated user's interest theme selections.

get.description: Fetch preferences, creating defaults if none exist.

post.summary: Record a walk session for the authenticated user.

405.description: Method not allowed; only GET probes are supported

get.summary:
Liveness probe. Return 200 while the process is marked alive and 503 once draining.
Call `HealthState::mark_unhealthy` before graceful shutdown to surface the drain early.

200.description: Server is ready to handle traffic

405.description: Method not allowed; only GET probes are supported

get.summary: Readiness probe. Return 200 when dependencies are initialized and the server can handle traffic; return 503 otherwise.

servers.description: Relative to the deployment base URL
